title: argyreia apoensis
intro: Argyreia apoensis, also known as the Apo Climbing Snail Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antivenom properties. The health benefits of Argyreia apoensis include pain relief, reduction of fever, and treatment of snakebites and other venomous bites. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
